title: Schedule 2: Refugees Outside Canada (IMM 0008 SCH2) - Canada.ca
content:
## Schedule 2: Refugees Outside Canada (IMM 0008 SCH2)
Schedule 2: Refugees Outside Canada [IMM 0008 SCH2] (PDF, 2.2 MB)

### How to download, save and open this form
- Use your computer. The form may not open on tablets or mobile phones .
- Save the form on your computer in a place you can remember. For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form. If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form.
- If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- After you download the form, open it using Adobe Acrobat Reader.

#### Open this form in Acrobat Reader
- Open Acrobat Reader. You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms. Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms.
- Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- Select “ File ” from the top menu.
- Find the location where you saved the form, click on the file, and click “ Open. ”
Read the step by step instructions on how to complete the form.

### General Application Information

#### Schedule 2 – Refugees Outside Canada (IMM 0008 Schedule 2)
(This form is available in PDF only)

#### This form must be completed by:
- you, the principal applicant;
- your spouse or common-law partner (whether accompanying you to Canada or not), and ;
- your dependent children aged 18 years or older (whether accompanying you to Canada or
                                not).
You can download, save and fill out the form on a computer.

#### General Application Information
Check the box to tell us if you are:
- the principal applicant; or
- Unique Client Identifier (UCI) or file number (if you know).

### Parts A and B: Case Narrative & Immigration status and
                                    settlement abilities
For parts A & B, read the questions carefully and provide as much information as
                            possible. Providing precise information will make it easier for us to process your
                            application.

### Part C: Additional Family information
You must list all family members on this form even if they are not
                            accompanying you to Canada, are deceased, presumed deceased or their whereabouts are
                            unknown.
If the family member is deceased, type “deceased” in the “present address and e-mail address”
                            box.
If the family member’s location is unknown, type “unknown” in the “present address and e-mail
                            address” box.
Provide the following information for yourself, your spouse (if applicable), your
                                    mother and father. Enter their:
- city/town and country of birth
You must list the following information for all of your children. Enter their:
- their relationship to you (e.g. daughter, son, step-daughter, adopted son)
- city/town and country of birth
Brothers and sisters
Provide the following information for all your brothers and sisters. Enter their:
- relationship to you (sister, brother, step-sister, step-brother, etc.)
- city/town and country of birth
- marital status (including common-law relationships) and
Provide the following information for any relatives you have in Canada. Enter
                                    their:
- relationship to you (aunt, uncle, brother, sister, cousin, etc.)
- current city and province of residence
This information is used for government-assisted refugees to help in matching them with a
                            destination in Canada.

### Part D: Consent and Declaration
- Tell us if someone helped you complete your application. This includes anyone
                                        who helped you answer the questions. If you answer “ Yes ”,
                                            write the name of the individual or organization that helped you.
If you answer “ Yes ”,
                                            write the name of the individual or organization that helped you.
- Tell us if you paid this person or organization to help you complete your form.
Declaration of applicant
Read the declaration, sign and date. If you do not sign, the application will
                                        be returned to you .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
